title: Lost in Work
author: Erica C. Barnett
subline: What is work for, and why are we doing so much of it?
customHTML:
<svg viewBox="0 0 1600 2400" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice" style="position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%">
  <!-- A maze. The walls are hairlines. A small red dot is the 'you', placed deep inside the maze, lost. A thin path leads to the entrance, but it's a long way. -->
  <g stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="2" fill="none">
    <!-- Outer wall -->
    <rect x="200" y="280" width="1200" height="1140"/>
    <!-- Internal walls -->
    <line x1="200" y1="500" x2="500" y2="500"/>
    <line x1="500" y1="500" x2="500" y2="800"/>
    <line x1="500" y1="800" x2="900" y2="800"/>
    <line x1="900" y1="800" x2="900" y2="500"/>
    <line x1="900" y1="500" x2="1200" y2="500"/>
    <line x1="600" y1="600" x2="600" y2="1000"/>
    <line x1="600" y1="1000" x2="1100" y2="1000"/>
    <line x1="1100" y1="1000" x2="1100" y2="700"/>
    <line x1="300" y1="1100" x2="700" y2="1100"/>
    <line x1="700" y1="1100" x2="700" y2="1300"/>
    <line x1="700" y1="1300" x2="1100" y2="1300"/>
  </g>
  <!-- A faint dotted path (the trail of lost wandering) -->
  <g fill="#cccccc">
    <circle cx="220" cy="600" r="3"/>
    <circle cx="240" cy="700" r="3"/>
    <circle cx="240" cy="850" r="3"/>
    <circle cx="380" cy="850" r="3"/>
    <circle cx="500" cy="850" r="3"/>
    <circle cx="500" cy="950" r="3"/>
    <circle cx="700" cy="950" r="3"/>
    <circle cx="700" cy="1100" r="3"/>
    <circle cx="900" cy="1100" r="3"/>
    <circle cx="900" cy="1200" r="3"/>
    <circle cx="1000" cy="1200" r="3"/>
  </g>
  <!-- The 'you' — red dot, deep in the maze -->
  <circle cx="1000" cy="1200" r="14" fill="#fc1c46"/>
  <text x="1020" y="1180" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#fc1c46" letter-spacing="0.18em">YOU</text>
  <!-- Entrance label -->
  <text x="200" y="260" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.18em">ENTRANCE</text>
  <!-- Top label -->
  <text x="800" y="220" text-anchor="middle" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.18em">THE WORK MAZE</text>
  <!-- Bottom label -->
  <text x="1400" y="1450" text-anchor="end" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.18em">EXIT (FAR)</text>
</svg>
rationale: An actual maze drawn in hairlines dominates the upper 2/3. The reader (you) is a small red dot lost deep inside, with a faint dotted trail of previous wandering visible. Below: 320px mixed-case 'Lost / in Work' title in weight 300. The visual metaphor is the book: capitalism's work is a maze with no clear exit, and most of us are somewhere in the middle, having wandered far from the entrance.
